input.headline: ‘We could have asked ChatGPT’: students fight back over course taught by AI

raw.headline: ‘We could have asked ChatGPT’: students fight back over course taught by AI

winner: The students were so outraged that the professor used AI to teach the class that they petitioned the dean to fire him, only to find out the dean had already replaced the entire faculty with a subscription to ChatGPT.

candidates.text: Why did the student bring a ladder to their AI-taught class? Because they heard the teacher was *raising* the bar—just like ChatGPT!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'raising the bar' and references to ChatGPT are clever and specific, fitting the headline well with a touch of surprise."}

candidates.text: Why did the student bring a ladder to class? Because their professor said the education was too *elevated*—it was powered by ChatGPT!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'elevated' are clever and specific to the AI education context; concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: I tried to ask my AI professor why the sky is blue, and he just said, "I can't see the sky—I'm a language model." Then I got an email: "We could have asked ChatGPT.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with AI's inability to see the sky, ties back to headline, concise and specific."}

candidates.text: The students were furious that the professor outsourced the syllabus to an AI, but they decided to take it out on him by making sure his final grade was strictly "artificial.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay with 'artificial' as a pun on both AI and the grade, good surprise factor and specificity."}

candidates.text: I tried to ask ChatGPT for help with my essay, but it turned out my professor was just waiting for me to say "We could have asked ChatGPT" and then gave me an F.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7, "reason": "The joke is specific and ties directly to the headline, with a surprising twist about the professor's reaction. However, it's slightly generic in its setup and lacks some punch."}

candidates.text: The students were so outraged that the professor used AI to teach the class that they petitioned the dean to fire him, only to find out the dean had already replaced the entire faculty with a subscription to ChatGPT.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with specific details, concise and funny, meets all constraints"}

candidates.text: Students protested when their professor said he was teaching the class because "it's what I'm good at." Then they realized the real issue was that he wasn't actually using ChatGPT—he just used his own notes.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and has a mild twist with the realization about ChatGPT. It ties into the headline well but lacks stronger punch or deeper surprise."}

candidates.text: The students were so offended by the professor's reliance on AI that they filed a formal grievance claiming he had violated the school's "No Outsourcing Your Personality" policy.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on AI outsourcing with a specific, humorous policy name; concise and natural."}

candidates.text: The professor assigned a paper on existentialism—turns out, the AI just wrote about its own lack of feelings.
